exemplar.title: How has the implementation of data analytics contributed to Farfetch establishing its market position due to customer engagement from 2021 - 2023?

detailedExemplarData.title: How has the implementation of data analytics contributed to Farfetch establishing its market position due to customer engagement from 2021 - 2023?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic and context are communicated accurately and effectively, with clear definitions of key terms and comprehensive background on Farfetchâs luxuryâecommerce environment.
Research question is explicitly stated, timeâbound and consistently referenced throughout the report, maintaining a clear line of inquiry.
Methodology outlines an appropriate range of secondary sources and analytical frameworks, demonstrating informed selection and alignment with the research question.
weaknesses: Research question phrasing introduces minor ambiguity between âmarket positionâ and âprofitabilityâ, and the study period is inconsistently referenced (2021â2023 vs. 2022â2023).
Methodology lacks explicit evaluation criteria for assessing the reliability and bias of secondary sources.
Some sections are underdeveloped (e.g., paragraph ending midâsentence) and require proofreading for continuity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work communicates the topic with precision and situates the research question clearly within its business context, meeting the highâlevel descriptors. Minor lapses in scope consistency and absence of explicit sourceâevaluation criteria prevent a full 6, placing this criterion solidly at 5 out of 6.

strengths: Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, with effective explanations of business concepts such as dynamic pricing and omnichannel tools.
Terminology and concepts (e.g., GMV, Adjusted EBITDA, predictive analytics) are used accurately and consistently throughout.
Source material (financial statements, industry statistics) is generally relevant and appropriately integrated into charts and tables to support discussion.
weaknesses: Several analytical models (e.g., Porterâs Five Forces) remain at a surface level and occasional misapplications (e.g., Ansoff quadrant misalignment) weaken conceptual depth.
Citation style is inconsistent and some data points (e.g., retention rates) lack proper sourcing or time frames.
Reliance on descriptive secondary commentary (e.g., podcast anecdote) without triangulation limits the depth of source application.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates clear knowledge and mostly appropriate application of specialist terminology and sources, but surfaceâlevel treatment of some frameworks and inconsistent citation practice prevent a higher band. A score of 4 out of 6 accurately reflects these strengths and shortcomings.

strengths: Research draws on a good breadth of secondary data sourcesâannual reports, Statista, academic journalsâand maintains clear relevance to the research question.
Analysis interprets quantitative trends (GMV, active customers) in light of dataâanalytics initiatives, with wellâsupported conclusions.
Argument is logically structured, uses multiple frameworks (SWOT, CPM) to compare strategic options, and includes a limitations section.
weaknesses: Depth of critical evaluation is limited: alternative explanations (e.g., macro-economic factors) are noted but not fully developed.
Some analytical points lack quantification (e.g., causal links between R&D spend and GMV) and counter-arguments remain modest.
Certain framework applications (Ansoff, substitute threat) would benefit from additional data to substantiate risk and barrier assessments.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall, the work demonstrates good, clearly relevant research and solid analytical interpretation, but falls short of sophisticated, consistently critical evaluation across all discussions. An 8 out of 12 reflects its position between clear and good criticalâthinking performance.

strengths: Structure is clearly appropriate: includes title page, abstract-style overview, contents, distinct sections, conclusion and appendices in correct order.
Layout and formatting adhere to guidelines: MLA citations, numbered tables/figures, word count and pagination are correctly applied.
Headings and navigation aids (e.g., section headers matching the contents page) enhance readability and professional presentation.
weaknesses: Table of contents shows inconsistent use of bold tags and slight font shifts in some headings.
Reference list exhibits varied hyperlink formatting and occasional missing publication/access dates.
Bibliography entries need uniform citation style and completeness for full professional consistency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation consistently meets all structural and formatting requirements with only minor inconsistencies that do not impede readability. This warrants the maximum score of 4 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question â âHow has the implementation of data analytics contributed to Farfetch establishing its market position due to customer engagement from 2021-2023?â â is explicitly stated, time-bounded and located within the introduction and conclusion, ensuring a continual line of inquiry.  
â¢ The question is generally well focused on a single firm, a specific practice (data analytics) and measurable outcomes (market position/customer engagement).  
â¢ Minor ambiguity arises because âmarket positionâ and âprofitabilityâ are occasionally treated interchangeably, and the scope widens slightly when the conclusion refers to 2022-23. These small lapses prevent full precision, keeping the strand at a solid 5 rather than 6.

The discussion is logically structured, progresses from quantitative to qualitative insights, and ends with a section on limitations, demonstrating some critical evaluation.  
â¢ The SWOT and CPM provide comparative views and the conclusion revisits the RQ.  
â¢ Evaluation, however, is often brief and tends to confirm rather than challenge findings; alternative interpretations (e.g., macro-economic factors) are acknowledged but not explored in depth, aligning with the lower end of the clear analysis band.

The essay exhibits clear knowledge of business management conceptsâAnsoff Matrix, Porterâs Five Forces, CPM, loyalty programmesâand links them to Farfetchâs strategic choices.  
â¢ Explanations of how dynamic pricing, omnichannel tools and acquisitions impact market share reflect coherent subject understanding.  
â¢ Yet several models (e.g., Porters 5 Forces risk ratings) stay surface-level and occasionally misapply weighting, preventing the consistently effective demonstration required for the top band.

Throughout the essay the student uses specialist language such as âGMVâ, âAdjusted EBITDAâ, âdynamic pricingâ, âpredictive analyticsâ and âcloud-based supply chainsâ accurately and in the correct context.  
â¢ Concept names are capitalised and defined before use, and formulae (e.g., gross profit margin) are provided.  
â¢ Minor slips (e.g., treating market share and market position synonymously) are present but do not impede overall precision, supporting a score of 5 within the highest band.

Analysis goes beyond description: GMV and active-customer trends are interpreted, links are made to dynamic pricing and exclusive drops, and frameworks (Ansoff, CPM) are applied to weigh strategic options.  
â¢ Most conclusions (e.g., data analytics driving 1 m incremental customers) are supported with numerical evidence or cited studies.  
â¢ Nevertheless, some leaps remain unquantified (e.g., exact causal relationship between R&D spend and GMV) and counter-arguments are modest, positioning the work mid-band.

Source material is largely relevant: financial statements, industry statistics and practitioner quotations are integrated into charts and tables that support subsequent discussion.  
â¢ Most figures are referenced and incorporated into commentary (e.g., linking GMV growth to analytics-led initiatives), showing appropriateness.  
â¢ However, some claims rely on secondary commentary without triangulation, and a few citations (e.g., podcast anecdote) remain descriptive rather than analytical, indicating âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ relevant application.

The methodology outlines the exclusive use of secondary data (annual reports, Statista, academic journals) and justifies choices by discussing reliability, bias control and alignment with IB tools.  
â¢ A range of quantitative indicators (GMV, GPM, active customers) and qualitative frameworks (Ansoff, Porter, CPM, SWOT) demonstrates informed selection appropriate to the RQ.  
â¢ Evidence of effectiveness is clear (e.g., cross-checking data, legal obligation of annual reports), but the plan lacks detail on source evaluation criteria and does not address potential gaps from the absence of primary data, so the work aligns with a convincing yet not entirely exemplary level 5.

Research covers financial statements, Statista time-series data, practitioner interviews and academic literature, all aligned to the RQ.  
â¢ Relevance is regularly signposted; for example, each quantitative indicator is explicitly linked to customer engagement or data-driven initiatives.  
â¢ The breadth is good but depth is limited by the absence of primary research and by relying on partial 2023 data, so the strand fits solidly in the âgood/clearâ band rather than excellent.

Formatting is consistently professional: MLA-style in-text citations, a comprehensive works-cited list, tables and figures numbered and captioned, and images referenced.  
â¢ Word count, page numbers and section spacing adhere to EE guidelines.  
â¢ Minor inconsistencies (occasional bold tags in the contents table, slight font size shifts) are negligible, so the work comfortably meets the highest layout expectations.

The student presents the topic and its business context in a concise title page, a clear abstract-style opening paragraph and a detailed introduction that explains Farfetchâs background, the relevance of data analytics, and the 2021-23 time frame.  
â¢ Key terms such as âdata analytics,â âmarket position,â and âcustomer engagementâ are explicitly defined and embedded in the narrative, demonstrating an assured grasp of what is being investigated.  
â¢ The explanation of why the issue matters to luxury e-commerce, supported by industry quotations and market statistics, gives the reader an immediate, comprehensive understanding of purpose and scope. Overall, communication is accurate, focused and complete, matching the highest-level descriptor.

A formal EE structure is evident: title page, abstract-style overview, contents page, introduction, methodology, distinct analytical sections, conclusion, works cited and appendices.  
â¢ Headings match the table of contents and pagination is accurate, ensuring logical navigation.  
â¢ All required elements are present and appropriately ordered, warranting the top presentation band.

annotations.comment: Applying acquisition examples (New Guards Group, Stadium Goods) effectively illustrates Farfetchâs incumbency advantage in industry analysis.
The Store of the Future initiative is well described; linking its real-time metrics (e.g., dwell times) to customer engagement metrics would enhance analytical depth.
Methodology outlines secondary sources but omits explicit evaluation criteria for source reliability and bias; specify how each source will be assessed against academic standards.
Citation style varies between parentheses and quotes; adopt a consistent author-date format (e.g., Harvard or MLA) and include page or paragraph numbers where applicable for clarity.
Analysis of the Ansoff matrix remains descriptive; integrate specific data analytics initiatives in each quadrant to deepen critical evaluation.
Threat of substitutes discussion lacks quantitative competitor performance; integrate competitor market share or revenue trends to assess substitute risk more rigorously.

The research question phrasing (âdue to customer engagementâ) introduces ambiguity in causality; refine wording to focus clearly on how data analytics influenced market position, ensuring terms like âmarket positionâ and âcustomer engagementâ are consistently defined and scoped.
The significance section articulates the studyâs contribution to business management and highlights practical applications for other luxury brands, demonstrating strong subject understanding.
Including the word count meets IB requirements and demonstrates compliance with formatting standards.
Threat of new entrants is justified qualitatively; quantify barriers by citing R&D or data infrastructure investments that raise entry costs.
Limitations acknowledge missing Q3/2023 data but do not discuss how this gap might bias trend conclusions; reflect on potential direction and magnitude of such bias.
Table of contents shows inconsistent use of bold tags for section entries; standardize heading styles and font weights for improved professional presentation.
Introduction effectively situates Farfetch in its luxury e-commerce context, providing clear background and rationale linked to the research question.
Paragraph ends mid-sentence, suggesting incomplete discussion; ensure all sections are fully developed and proofread for continuity.
Scope justification is clear but lacks critical reflection on the limitations of using solely secondary data without primary research to validate findings.

Growth in GMV is described qualitatively; include a calculation for compound annual growth rate using 
$$\text{CAGR}=\Bigl(\frac{1,964,275}{1,923,415}\Bigr)^{\!1/2}-1$$to quantify the increase.
Reference list shows inconsistent hyperlink formatting and line breaks; apply a uniform citation style (e.g., MLA or APA) throughout for professional presentation.
Including the gross profit margin formula enhances conceptual clarity and demonstrates correct use of business math tools.
Margin decline is attributed to cost increases without quantifying the exact percentage change in fulfillment costs; include supporting cost data to substantiate this link.
The 90% retention rate is asserted without a citation or time-frame; provide the source and define the period over which retention was measured.
Bargaining power of buyers is conflated with price sensitivity; distinguish between price-setting ability and customer switching behavior using market data.
Critical success factor weights in the CPM lack justification; explain the criteria for assigning weights to ensure scoring transparency.
Several bibliography entries omit publication dates or access dates; ensure all sources include complete citation details for proper referencing.
The âActive Customersâ section header is clearly labelled, matching the table of contents and aiding navigation.
Classifying acquisitions under âMarket Penetrationâ misaligns with the Ansoff framework; reposition examples to reflect âDiversificationâ or âMarket Developmentâ quadrants accurately.
Reliance on a podcast anecdote strengthens narrative but lacks triangulation; supplement with quantitative performance metrics to validate its impact.
Diversification discussion omits potential digital platform risks (e.g., system integration challenges); including these would provide a more balanced evaluation.
The Porterâs Five Forces diagram is clearly rendered with risk ratings and enhances conceptual understanding of industry structure.
Claims about supplier negotiation leverage lack empirical support; include price change data or supplier contract terms to substantiate medium-impact assessment.
Conclusion references a 2022â2023 period, whereas the introduction covers 2021â2023; align the study period consistently to maintain research focus.
